rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL ET AL VS. ANNE KIHAGI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Terminate The Ccsf Receivership And For An Order Directing Receiver To File Final Report & Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT JULIA MWANGI, CHRISTINE MWANGI, XELAN PROP 1, LLC, RENKA PROP, LLC, NOZARI 2, LLC, ZORIALL, LLC MOTION TO TERMINATE THE CCSF RECEIVERSHIP AND FOR AN ORDER DIRECTING RECEIVER TO FILE FINAL REPORT & ACCOUNTING is continued to May 16, 2024. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALTO TIBURON AVC, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL VS. DIMITRI AGAPITOS RIGOPOULOS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2024 line 4. DEFENDANT DIMITRI RIGOPOULOS, CHRISTOS RIGOPOULOS, THE RIGOPOULOS GROUP, INC., VANGUARD PROPERTIES, INC. NOTICE OF DEMURRER AND DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to address in good faith all issues raised by the demurrer. The Court notes that some arguments advanced in the moving papers are not addressed in the opposition, suggesting that Plaintiff believes them to be well-taken. Therefore, Plaintiff shall address those issues in the amended complaint. The Plaintiff shall have the final leave to amend to allege facts supporting non-speculative damages. Allegations currently contained in paragraphs 39, 47, 55 and 61 are conclusory and speculative. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ELIAS SHAMASH VS. PATRICK SMITH ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant Patrick Smith
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2024 line 6. PLAINTIFF ELIAS SHAMASH NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY, TO ESTABLISH ADMISSIONS FROM DEFENDANT, AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST DEFENDANT PATRICK SMITH is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion to deem admissions admitted is DENIED, moot. Responses provided. Motion to Compel discovery responses is GRANTED. Defendant to provide complete verified responses to (i) Form Interrogatories - General (Set One), (ii) Form Interrogatories - Unlawful Detainer (Set One), (iii) Specially Prepared Interrogatories (Set One), and (iv) Requests for Production of Documents and Tangible Things (Set One) within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Sanctions granted in the amount of $660 if responses are not provided as ordered above. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ALI A. AMINI ET AL VS. ANDREW LINTON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 1, 2024 line 7. DEFENDANT ANGELINA GREEP MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS is GRANTED. Plaintiff failed to show that the process server exercised reasonable diligence to personally serve Ms. Greep before resorting to substitute service. Plaintiff cites no authority for the proposition that a 2-hour steak-out on a Saturday morning satisfies CCP 415.20 reasonable diligence requirement. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
